refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below target, except during safety-critical interventions.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry for performance tracking.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical actuation timing; minimize travel path via route planning; parallelize load/dump operations where safe.
DP-B: Implement predictive cycle time modeling with real-time adjustment of operational parameters (speed, acceleration profiles) based on site conditions and duty cycle.
DP-C: Establish teleoperation control architecture with low-latency network link and deterministic command processing; implement safety-aware task scheduling.
DP-D: Integrate cycle time KPI monitoring into telemetry pipeline with continuous p95 aggregation and alerting on deviation from CT_target.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B + DP-C + DP-D (integrated): Implement a closed-loop cycle time management system comprising: (1) predictive cycle time modeling with real-time parameter adjustment (vehicle speed, acceleration, dwell times) based on duty cycle and site conditions; (2) low-latency teleoperation control with deterministic command processing and safety-aware task scheduling; (3) continuous telemetry-based p95 cycle time monitoring with automated alerting and performance dashboards for site manager and operator feedback.

potential_sources: Coupling between vehicle actuation timing (DP-B mechanical) and teleoperation latency (DP-C network/control): mitigated by explicit latency budget allocation and deterministic scheduling.
Coupling between safety services and cycle time optimization (DP-B parameter adjustment): mitigated by safety-aware task scheduling that preserves safety interlocks.
Coupling between telemetry reporting (DP-D) and real-time control loop (DP-B/C): mitigated by separate telemetry pipeline with bounded latency.
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP is a composite solution addressing functional, control, and observability dimensions. Couplings are identified and mitigation strategies are embedded in the DP definition. No circular dependencies detected.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic/dynamic constraints and site conditions (e.g., terrain, load mass).
ASSUMPTION: Teleoperation network link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency performance to support real-time control without exceeding allocated budget.
ASSUMPTION: Safety services can be integrated into task scheduling without blocking critical cycle time paths.
RISK: If site conditions vary significantly (e.g., mud, steep grades), predictive model may require frequent re-tuning; mitigation: adaptive model with on-site calibration.
RISK: Teleoperation latency spikes (network congestion, packet loss) could violate cycle time target; mitigation: fallback to autonomous mode with conservative timing.
RISK: Safety interventions (e.g., emergency stop, stability checks) may introduce unpredictable delays; mitigation: pre-compute safety margins into cycle time budget.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly define the predictive model inputs (duty cycle parameters, site conditions, load mass, terrain type) and the real-time adjustment algorithm (e.g., PID control, model predictive control). Specify latency budget allocation across teleoperation (DP-C) and safety services. Define p95 aggregation window and alerting thresholds for DP-D.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for orchestrating vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to achieve cycle time KPI. Owns the predictive cycle time model, real-time parameter adjustment logic, telemetry aggregation pipeline, and performance dashboard. Interfaces with Vehicle for actuation commands and status; with Teleoperation for remote control and latency management; with Safety Services for safety-aware task scheduling; and with external stakeholders via Telemetry and Tasking interfaces.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Continuous emission of cycle time metrics (load time, travel time, dump time, total cycle time, p95 aggregates) and performance alerts to site manager dashboard and remote monitoring systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Reception of duty cycle parameters, site conditions, and operational targets (CT_target, load mass, terrain type) from site manager or planning system to inform predictive model and parameter adjustment.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication with Safety Services: NGHE Solution sends cycle time optimization parameters for safety validation; Safety Services returns safety constraints and approved parameter ranges to prevent unsafe acceleration/speed profiles.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Underlying network connectivity for teleoperation control commands and telemetry data transmission; NGHE Solution monitors latency and adjusts control timing accordingly.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of cycle time KPI management. It integrates vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. Cycle time modeling, real-time adjustment, and telemetry aggregation are cohesive functions that belong at the system level. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry output and tasking input cross module boundaries.

Vehicle owns actuation timing and mechanical execution, but cycle time optimization logic (predictive modeling, parameter adjustment) should reside at NGHE Solution level to coordinate across teleoperation and safety constraints. If contained in Vehicle, would require Vehicle to own teleoperation latency and safety scheduling logic, creating high diffusion and coupling.

Teleoperation owns control latency and command processing, but cycle time KPI is a system-level metric that depends on vehicle execution and safety constraints. If contained in Teleoperation, would require ownership of vehicle actuation timing and safety scheduling, creating unnatural coupling.

Safety Services owns safety constraints and interlocks, but cycle time optimization is a performance objective, not a safety function. If contained in Safety Services, would blur the distinction between safety and performance, creating confusion and potential conflicts in prioritization.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because: (1) cycle time KPI is a system-level acceptance criterion that requires integration across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety domains; (2) predictive modeling and real-time adjustment logic are cohesive and belong at the system orchestration level; (3) telemetry aggregation and performance reporting are system-level observability functions; (4) interfaces with external stakeholders (site manager, planning system) are naturally at the system level.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a 'god module' with too many responsibilities, it may become difficult to test and maintain. MITIGATION: Decompose into sub-modules at next level (e.g., CycleTimeOptimizer, TelemetryAggregator, ParameterAdjustmentController) with clear ownership and interfaces.

RISK: Cycle time optimization may require real-time feedback from Vehicle and Teleoperation that is not available through standard interfaces. MITIGATION: Define explicit telemetry and control interfaces with bounded latency and deterministic delivery.

RISK: Safety Services may impose constraints that conflict with cycle time targets, requiring negotiation and trade-off analysis. MITIGATION: Establish safety-aware task scheduling that preserves safety interlocks while optimizing cycle time within approved parameter ranges.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the internal architecture of NGHE Solution: define sub-modules for cycle time modeling, real-time parameter adjustment, telemetry aggregation, and performance dashboard. Specify the interfaces between these sub-modules and with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Define the latency budget and determinism requirements for each interface to ensure cycle time KPI is achievable.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (predictive cycle time modeling + real-time parameter adjustment + low-latency teleoperation + continuous monitoring) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target with monitoring and reporting). DP is comprehensive, addressing functional execution (vehicle timing), control (teleoperation latency), safety integration, and observability (telemetry). No functional gaps identified.

diffusion_risks: Cycle time optimization logic is centralized in NGHE Solution, but depends on real-time feedback from Vehicle (actuation status, timing) and Teleoperation (latency, command processing). Interfaces must be well-defined and deterministic to avoid diffusion of responsibility.

Safety Services must provide approved parameter ranges to NGHE Solution; if safety constraints are not clearly communicated, NGHE Solution may attempt unsafe optimizations. Mitigation: explicit SafetyAlert interface with structured constraint definitions.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate container for cycle time KPI management. Containment score of 95 reflects strong cohesion and minimal diffusion. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and necessary for system integration. No artificial module boundaries or unnecessary diffusion detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is measurable via telemetry (p95 aggregation). Verification requires instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle. Acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target) are objective and testable. NGHE Solution ownership enables end-to-end verification and performance tracking.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution as the system-level owner. Sub-allocations to Vehicle (actuation timing), Teleoperation (latency budget), and Safety Services (constraint integration) are clear and traceable. No ambiguity in responsibility or verification authority.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit latency budget allocation across Teleoperation and Safety Services to ensure cycle time target is achievable.
Specify the predictive cycle time model inputs, outputs, and update frequency (e.g., per-cycle, per-duty-cycle, adaptive).
Define the p95 aggregation window (e.g., rolling 100-cycle window) and alerting thresholds for performance monitoring.
Establish safety-aware task scheduling algorithm that preserves safety interlocks while optimizing cycle time within approved parameter ranges.
Clarify the fallback strategy if teleoperation latency exceeds budget (e.g., autonomous mode with conservative timing).

decision.justification: Refined FR-NGHE-002 is well-defined and addresses the cycle time KPI requirement with explicit measurement and monitoring. Selected DP is comprehensive and addresses functional, control, and observability dimensions. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM owner with high containment score and minimal diffusion. However, the DP is composite and requires decomposition into sub-modules and sub-functions to enable detailed design and verification. Specifically, the predictive cycle time model, real-time parameter adjustment logic, telemetry aggregation, and safety-aware task scheduling are distinct sub-functions that require further refinement at the next decomposition level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below target, except during safety-critical interventions.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry for performance tracking.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic/dynamic constraints and site conditions.
Teleoperation network link provides sufficient bandwidth and latency performance.
Safety services can be integrated into task scheduling without blocking critical cycle time paths.
Predictive cycle time model can be calibrated on-site for varying site conditions.
Telemetry pipeline has bounded latency and does not interfere with real-time control loop.
Site manager and operator will provide timely feedback on cycle time performance for model refinement.
open_risks: Site conditions may vary significantly, requiring frequent model re-tuning.
Teleoperation latency spikes could violate cycle time target; fallback strategy needed.
Safety interventions may introduce unpredictable delays; safety margins must be pre-computed.
NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if not properly decomposed; sub-module architecture must be defined.
Interfaces between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services must be deterministic and well-defined.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of predictive cycle time model (inputs, outputs, algorithm, update frequency).
Latency budget allocation across Teleoperation and Safety Services with justification.
Safety-aware task scheduling algorithm with proof of safety preservation.
Telemetry aggregation pipeline design with latency and determinism guarantees.
Field trial results demonstrating p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under representative duty cycle.
Safety event correlation analysis showing no safety compromise from cycle time optimization.
